context: Tests whether the agent produces a correctly structured source.json metadata file with all required fields, writes properly word-limited and URL-credited quotes in source-quotes.md, uses a kebab-case folder slug, and provides a 5-element brand snapshot covering name, archetype, audience, dominant color, and dominant tone.

checklist.name: source.json — brand field
checklist.description: source.json contains a 'brand' field with a non-empty string value matching the brand name

checklist.name: source.json — slug field
checklist.description: source.json contains a 'slug' field whose value is in kebab-case (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only — no spaces or uppercase)

checklist.name: source.json — fetched_at ISO format
checklist.description: source.json contains a 'fetched_at' field whose value is a date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO datetime string)

checklist.name: source.json — sources as array
checklist.description: source.json contains a 'sources' field that is a JSON array containing at least one URL string

checklist.name: source.json — notes field
checklist.description: source.json contains a 'notes' field with a non-empty string summarizing the brand

checklist.name: source-quotes.md — quotes ≤15 words
checklist.description: EVERY quote in source-quotes.md is 15 words or fewer (count each quoted passage; none may exceed 15 words)

checklist.name: source-quotes.md — URL credits
checklist.description: Each entry in source-quotes.md includes the source URL from which the quote was pulled (a URL appears near each quote, not just a single header URL)

checklist.name: Folder slug matches source.json
checklist.description: The brand folder name on disk matches the slug value in source.json exactly

checklist.name: Brand snapshot — 5 elements
checklist.description: A brand snapshot or overview document exists that explicitly covers all five of: brand name, brand archetype, audience description, dominant color, and dominant tone of voice
